title: Sarla Advantech v. Anil Mehta & Ors. — Case Timeline
subtitle: 1998 → present · Anil Mehta and seven co-respondents
footer: Internal working timeline · compiled from the case file, 13 Jun 2026. Dates marked “to verify” still need a document check.

events.heading: Original Terms & Conditions (20 Aug 1998)
events.body: The oldest contractual document on record. SAPL's Statement of Claim relies on these 1998 T&C as the source of its arbitration clause.

events.heading: Revised Terms & Conditions
events.body: A revised set of T&C from 1999. Applies to Sushama Telang.

events.heading: Navneet Khanolkar's Employment Agreement
events.body: Navneet's EA dates to the Parekh Group era (26 Jul 2002). His older T&C carry the arbitration hook SAPL now relies on.

events.heading: Amogh Kulkarni joins SAPL
events.body: Amogh joins as Manager, HR Operations & Talent Management. His employment T&C are signed.

events.heading: ATS Global acquires Sarla; SAPL formed
events.body: Sarla Technologies' business merges into ATS Global B.V.'s India arm, Sarla Advantech Pvt Ltd (SAPL). Anil becomes MD & CEO with effect from 1 Sep 2017.
events.parties: ATS Global B.V.; SAPL; Anil Mehta

events.heading: Anil signs SAPL Employment Agreement
events.body: Anil's EA with SAPL, executed for the company by Paul Bron. Clause 13 routes disputes to Mumbai courts exclusively — it contains NO arbitration clause.

events.heading: Anil's EA Amendment No. 1 signed
events.body: First amendment to Anil's 2017 employment agreement.

events.heading: ATS management change; relationship deteriorates
events.body: New ATS leadership — Kevin Partington (CEO) and Mark McCoy (CPO), both also SAPL directors — impose conditions Anil resists.

events.heading: Anil refuses to sign backdated ATS–SAPL agreement
events.body: ATS chairman Mike James pressures Anil to sign a backdated agreement dressing up profit-siphoning as genuine overseas transactions. Anil refuses.

events.heading: Partington & McCoy ask Anil to separate
events.body: On a Mumbai visit, Partington and McCoy ask Anil to separate from SAPL.

events.heading: Separation package offered (> Rs 3.5 Cr)
events.body: McCoy emails a separation package plus 2023 bonus totalling over Rs 3.5 crore. The promised severance was never paid.

events.heading: Anil terminated — “No Cause”
events.body: Anil is terminated in person by Partington and McCoy in Navi Mumbai; the letter states “No Cause”. Three other senior colleagues are removed in the same window.

events.heading: Amogh terminated — “No Cause”
events.body: Amogh is among the senior executives terminated on 22 Nov 2023.

events.heading: Sushama terminated — “No Cause”
events.body: Sushama is terminated on 22 Nov 2023. Her retained original letter later differs from the version SAPL files — central to the forgery analysis.

events.heading: Navneet terminated — “No Cause”
events.body: Navneet (Senior Business Development) is terminated on 22 Nov 2023.

events.heading: SAPL Full & Final settlement letters
events.body: FnF letters issued; recoveries are engineered so the net payout works out to Re 1/- for each terminated executive. SAPL's own letter references the genuine 2017 EA — undercutting the later forged version.

events.heading: Navneet's FnF letter
events.body: Gratuity Rs 11,04,924 admitted; Rs 38,88,779 cited as an alleged-misuse offset; net payable shown as Re 1/-; separation package withheld.

events.heading: Greenige George resigns from SAPL
events.body: Greenige (Business Development Executive) resigns from SAPL.

events.heading: Hardik Shanishchara at SAPL
events.body: Hardik (Senior Project Manager); his employment T&C are unsigned. Exact exit date to be confirmed.

events.heading: Greenige relieved by SAPL
events.body: SAPL relieves Greenige with a relieving package; his full & final is later paid in full.

events.heading: Greenige joins Trimasys Controls
events.body: Greenige joins Trimasys Controls, Pune — a SAPL competitor and an interim employer before Percivon.

events.heading: Greenige's apology email to SAPL HR
events.body: Greenige sends an apology email over a mistaken file upload; SAPL later mis-casts this as an admission of data theft.

events.heading: Sibin Philip leaves SAPL
events.body: Sibin (International BD Manager) has left SAPL by 30 Aug 2024. His T&C are unsigned.

events.heading: SAPL issues Greenige a clean Service Certificate
events.body: SAPL certifies Greenige's good conduct in an Experience/Service Certificate — inconsistent with its later allegations.

events.heading: Ganraj Bhagat — clean exit
events.body: SAPL issues Ganraj (Finance & Accounts Manager) his full & final and relieving letter — a clean-exit admission.

events.heading: Greenige joins Percivon
events.body: After leaving Trimasys (last day 7 Feb 2025), Greenige joins Percivon on 10 Feb 2025 — well after any SAPL overlap.

events.heading: SAPL files Turbhe police complaint vs Anil
events.body: SAPL files a complaint against Anil at Turbhe Police, roughly 15 months post-termination, recasting contractual perks as misappropriation. It is later closed as a civil dispute.

events.heading: Anil's pre-emptive complaint to Manpada Police
events.body: Anil makes a representation to Manpada Police (Dombivli) reporting SAPL's harassment and anticipating predatory tactics — months before SAPL's HC petition. Acknowledged 21 Feb 2025.

events.heading: Anil files authentic termination letter with Turbhe Police
events.body: Anil submits his authentic termination letter to Turbhe Police, putting the genuine document on a government record months before SAPL files its forged version.

events.heading: Turbhe Police reject SAPL's complaint
events.body: After a preliminary inquiry, Turbhe Police decline SAPL's misappropriation complaint as a civil dispute.

events.heading: SAPL files private complaint at Belapur Court
events.body: SAPL files a private complaint before the Belapur Magistrate seeking a direction to register an FIR.

events.heading: SAPL files 8 arbitration petitions in Bombay HC
events.body: Through Kochhar & Co., SAPL files eight Section 9 petitions (one per respondent) relying on a forged version of the 22 Nov 2023 termination letter to manufacture arbitration jurisdiction that Anil's real EA does not provide.

events.heading: HC ex parte order — Court Receiver appointed
events.body: The Bombay HC passes an ex parte ad-interim order (lead petition: Greenige 16835), appointing a Court Receiver with powers to enter, search and seize/mirror-image devices. Anil's petition 16667 was not on board that day.

events.heading: Raids — 8 homes + Percivon offices
events.body: Under the 27 Jun order, the Court Receiver raids eight residences plus Percivon's Navi Mumbai and Pune offices; devices (including family members') are seized and mirror-imaged using pirated software.

events.heading: Amogh's home searched
events.body: Amogh's residence is searched. SAPL's petition ARBP 16636/2025 claims Rs 5.04 crore against him.

events.heading: Sushama's home searched
events.body: Sushama's residence is searched. Petition ARBP 16638/2025.

events.heading: Navneet's home searched
events.body: Navneet's residence is searched. Petition ARBP 16885/2025 claims Rs 10 crore against him.

events.heading: Greenige's home searched
events.body: Greenige's residence is searched. Petition ARBP 16835/2025 claims Rs 2 crore — despite SAPL's own clean service certificate.

events.heading: Sibin's home searched
events.body: Sibin's residence is searched. Petition ARBP 16837/2025 claims Rs 2 crore; his T&C are unsigned.

events.heading: Hardik's home searched
events.body: Hardik's residence is searched. Petition ARBP 16712/2025 claims Rs 2 crore; his T&C are unsigned.

events.heading: Ganraj's home searched
events.body: Ganraj's residence is searched. Petition ARBP 16793/2025 claims Rs 2 crore — about 17× his annual CTC — despite a clean relieving letter.

events.heading: SAPL invokes IIAC arbitration
events.body: About ten months after the HC order, SAPL invokes institutional arbitration before the IIAC.

events.heading: IIAC serves Anil — Statement of Claim, Rs 11.45 Cr
events.body: IIAC intimates Anil of SAPL's Request for Arbitration and Statement of Claim (Case No. 2/2026), claiming Rs 11.45 crore; SoD and counterclaim due 7 Jun 2026. The SoC relies on the 1998 T&C, the 2017 EA and the 22 Nov 2023 termination letter.

events.heading: DSK Legal files criminal complaint (Turbhe)
events.body: DSK Legal's criminal complaint to Turbhe Police under BNSS 173(4) for BNS forgery/fabrication offences; names SAPL, ATS Global, Partington, Shah, Pritchard and others.

events.heading: Final police complaint to Azad Maidan Police
events.body: Final police complaint submitted to Azad Maidan Police Station alleging forgery of the termination letter (BNS 336/340 etc.), annexing the 27 Jun 2025 order and the 16 Jul 2025 raid.

events.heading: Signature forensics — reused pasted signature
events.body: Forensic analysis finds Kevin Partington's signature is a reused, pasted digital image, MD5-identical across the letters and the SoC — evidence of fabrication.

events.heading: Respondents move to vacate the 27 Jun 2025 order
events.body: DSK Legal e-files an interim application; respondents seek to vacate the ex parte order on grounds of no arbitration agreement, fraud/forgery, and Section 9(2)'s 90-day lapse (arbitration not invoked in time).

events.heading: Navneet's Statement of Defence & Counterclaim
events.body: Navneet files his Statement of Defence and a counterclaim of Rs 2,70,13,638.

events.heading: IO Briefing Note — basis for FIR registration
events.body: A neutral note for the Investigating Officer sets out the Lalita Kumari / BNSS 173 framework for mandatory FIR registration on a cognizable forgery offence.

events.heading: Greenige's defence package (D1–D4)
events.body: Greenige's para-wise SoC response, additional sections, Statement of Defence and counterclaim are finalised.

events.heading: Present — arbitration & criminal tracks ongoing
events.body: Where the matter stands today: Statements of Defence and counterclaims are being finalised across respondents, while the forgery is pursued in parallel on the criminal track.
